currentState: Jaina and Cyndia now operate as practical protectee and practical protector whenever the room narrows into route management. The intimacy remains minimal, but the care is real and procedural.
powerDynamic: Jaina still owns the magical problem. Cyndia still owns corridor safety, readiness, and the body-buffer that keeps Jaina from having to solve two kinds of danger at once.

stateHistory.currentState: Guarded detention. Cyndia treats Jaina as a contained anomaly rather than a conventional prisoner, because the mage's behavior is too cooperative for sabotage and too disruptive to ignore.
Watchful escort professionalism. Cyndia still handles Jaina as a controlled risk, but the relationship has shifted from static detention to active field protection.
Operational head-start pair. Cyndia is no longer merely Jaina’s escorting flank; chapter 15 makes her the left-hand field support for Jaina’s source plan.
Chapter 20 turns them into a sea-crossing support pair. Cyndia cannot solve the supernatural problem, but she remains part of the human structure that keeps Jaina from being alone inside it.

Practical support pair carried onto the sea. Chapter 20 keeps Cyndia as the useful edge around Jaina's dangerous brilliance, helping absorb the ordinary hardship of the crossing while the mage spends herself on the extraordinary one.

Chapter 22 widens the relationship from support into active protection under collapse risk. Cyndia still cannot solve Frostmourne, but she is now visibly part of the body-buffer around Jaina while the mage spends herself into trembling and near-fall.

Chapter 23 keeps the relationship in outer-ring protection. Cyndia does not enter the throne-room core, but her silent corridor work and automatic inclusion of Jaina in the protected shell confirm that the mage now counts as one of the people Cyndia guards rather than merely one of the people she escorts.

physicalDynamics.pattern: Cyndia's side of the relationship is all evidence handling: staff confiscated, satchel secured, prisoner moved out of earshot of the road.
Jaina does not fight physically or attempt escape, forcing Cyndia to deal with an unusual kind of detainee — one more interested in continuing her work than in freedom.

Once the ride begins, Cyndia's physical relationship to Jaina becomes positional: she keeps the mage in formation, warns her to stay in the saddle, and covers the treeline while Jaina kneels to read the road.
By chapter 15 they move as practical partners rather than guard and detainee: Jaina naming targets over the wind while Cyndia matches pace and answers with gate and lane options.
Cyndia opens the first lane with her horse and keeps calling routes while Jaina, exhausted and stunned, obeys on the strength of motion rather than will.
Once on the wall they do not pair emotionally, but they do pair functionally: Jaina at the parapet, Cyndia at the outward angle keeping the line survivable.
At the base of the wall and on the road, Cyndia's side of the relationship becomes pure readiness: horses already made ready, water already there, no wasted questions before movement resumes.
On the Wolfheart the relationship is mostly indirect support: Cyndia enduring the deck and staying useful while Jaina turns outward toward the source and the strain it places on her body.

Cyndia’s chapter22 presence is all flank and catch-geometry: moving with Jaina on the return, widening the protective sweep to include her without ceremony, and staying close enough that collapse never becomes a solitary event.
By the city approach, Cyndia is working the practical shell around Jaina’s failing body — space, pace, and angle — while Jaina burns everything inward on the anti-blade sequence.

Chapter 23 expresses the bond almost entirely through signal and spacing: Cyndia’s hand-count at the branch, her held lane in the passage, and the automatic way Jaina is placed inside the protected geometry.

nameUsage.cyndiaCallsJaina: Still sparse and practical; the chapter keeps the care in silent route work more than speech.
nameUsage.jainaCallsCyndia: Minimal and functional. Trust is present as acceptance of Cyndia’s handling rather than verbal warmth.

history.event: Cyndia catches Jaina at the second ring with spellwork layered over the ward runes and detains her without reported violence.
Cyndia becomes the first person in chapter 1 to mediate Jaina's warning into a form Sylvanas can evaluate.
Cyndia physically participates in Jaina's release by dropping the ward-circle and returning her to the road under escort rather than to ordinary freedom.
During the wildlife attack and its aftermath, Cyndia helps keep Jaina alive long enough to continue diagnosing the contamination.
Cyndia joins Jaina on the head-start ride to Stratholme, turning their connection from guarded field reliance into direct operational collaboration.
Their shouted exchange on the road makes clear that Cyndia is now helping think through routes, gates, and movement flow inside Jaina’s plan rather than merely keeping the mage upright in the saddle.
Cyndia gets Jaina off the gate line by calling the service-side route before shock can freeze her in place.
The chapter extends their chapter-15 head-start collaboration from source-run logistics into shared witness logistics.
Cyndia has horses, water, and tack ready below the wall, turning care into operational continuity and helping carry Jaina from witness paralysis back into road motion.

The crossing to Northrend keeps Cyndia in the familiar support role under unfamiliar conditions: not the one driving the answer, but still one of the people who help make Jaina's continued pursuit physically possible.

The return from Northrend makes Cyndia’s old escort logic reappear in a transformed way: not guarding a detainee, but helping carry a half-collapsing ally through dock, road, and palace approach without fanfare.
Chapter22 confirms Cyndia’s protective sweep now visibly includes Jaina, making the relationship’s current truth one of adopted field-care rather than mere adjacent usefulness.
At the service approach Cyndia silently signals the occupied branch and helps preserve the corridor lane while Jaina and Sylvanas move on the chamber.

trajectory: Guarded escort -> operational run pair -> witness-and-recovery support line -> sea-crossing support pair -> chapter 22’s active protective shell around Jaina. -> chapter 23’s corridor-guarded threshold work.
